speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mere paas rone ka waqt nahi hai kyunki mujhe login karke bacchon ki school fees ke liye extra shift lagani hai
text: Office pantry mein unhe mere accent ka mazak udate suna toh dil baith gaya, par mere paas rone ka waqt nahi hai kyunki mujhe login karke bacchon ki school fees ke liye extra shift lagani hai. Widowed hone ke baad samaj pehle hi ungli uthata hai, isliye main bas apna sar jhuka kar kaam karti hoon kyunki mera rukna poore khandaan ki 'izzat' aur financial stability ke liye khatra hai. Thakan itni hai ki sharir toot raha hai, par 'farz' nibhane ke liye mujhe har haal mein bas machine ki tarah chalte rehna hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the immense duty and role burden that forces her to suppress her pain using Lens B.
text: Baccho ki fees aur ghar ki izzat bachane ke is farz ke aage aapne apne dukh aur thakan ko bilkul piche rakh diya hai, aur khud ko ek machine ki tarah aage badhne par majboor kar diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, kyunki agar main ek pal ko bhi ruki, toh sab log bolenge dekho widow hai, akele bacchon ko nahi paal sakti. Khandaan ka naam kharab ho jayega aur us financial stability ka kya jo itni mehnat se banayi hai?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the feared consequence of losing her hard-earned stability and family respect using Lens E.
text: Aisa mehsoos hota hai ki agar aap ek pal ke liye bhi dagmagayi, toh sab kuch bikhar jayega aur samaj khandaan ki us izzat par sawal uthayega jise aap akele bacha rahi hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul. Ek din bhi chhutti lene se lagta hai mere bacchon ka future compromise ho jayega. Farz nibhane ki is daud mein mujhe rone tak ka waqt nahi milta, bas screen ke aage extra shift karni padti hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Asking a question to gently explore her internal state beneath the relentless duty.
text: Is farz ko nibhane ki daud mein, jab aap khud ko lagatar extra shifts aur expectations ke beech pisti hui mehsoos karti hain, toh andar hi andar kaisa lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Andar se toh puri tarah khokhli ho chuki hoon. Jab office pantry mein wo log mere tier-2 city accent par has rahe the, laga wahin roun. Par ek widow aur single mother ko kamzor dikhne ki permission nahi hoti society mein.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Softly mirroring the relational burden of the societal stigma against a widowed mother using Lens C.
text: Ek widow hone ke naate jaise samaj ne aapke samne rone par bhi pabandi laga di hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, akele hi uthana padta hai apna aasu. Mere husband ke jaane ke baad se kisi ne nahi poocha main kaisi hoon. Bas sabne yahi sikhaya ki 'farz' nibhana tumhara kaam hai ab, bacchon ke liye jeena hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting the long duration of carrying this immense responsibility entirely alone using Lens D.
text: Itne saalon se bina kisi sahare ke, aap lagatar apne is farz aur ghar ko akele hi apne kandhon par uthati aa rahi hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Uthana hi padega. Mere bacchon ko kisi ke aage haath nahi phailana padna chahiye, unki dignity zaruri hai. Par sach kahun? Mera sharir ab bilkul toot raha hai is boj ke tale. Aankhein jalne lagti hain night shifts mein.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting the unexpressed exhaustion of bearing the financial duty and identity burden, per the C4/C5/C6 + Duty_Based rule.
text: Lagta hai is 'izzat' aur akelepan ki zimmedariyon ko lagatar nibhane ki is endless struggle mein aap andar se is qadar exhaust ho chuki hain ki ab aapka poora jism bhi aapse aaram maang raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mere paas rone ka waqt nahi hai kyunki mujhe login karke bacchon ki school fees ke liye extra shift lagani hai
text: Aaram kaise karun? Jaisa maine pehle bhi kaha, mere paas rone ka waqt nahi hai kyunki mujhe login karke bacchon ki school fees ke liye extra shift lagani hai. Kal unki fees jama karne ki aakhiri date hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validating her immense dedication to her children while separating her worth from the societal expectations of a widow.
text: Aap jis tarah se akele apne bacchon ke future aur ghar ki dignity ko itni thakan mein bhi bacha kar rakhe hue hain, wo aapki bohot badi himmat hai; samaj chahe jo expectations rakhe, par aapki value sirf in zimmedariyon tak simmit nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Himmat ka toh pata nahi, par darr hamesha rehta hai. Aaj office mein accent ki wajah se mazak uda toh mujhe apne hi upar guilt hua ki shayad main is corporate culture ke layak hi nahi hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Using a brief systemic anecdote to normalize the class/regional bias in corporate spaces so she feels less personally responsible.
text: Corporate offices mein jahan tier-1 cities ka culture zyada dikhta hai, wahan accent ko lekar logo ko aise hi nicha dikhana bohot common hai — sirf aapke sath nahi ho raha, lekin aam taur par koi is discrimination ke baare mein bolega nahi kyunki job khone ka darr sabko rehta hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, job khone ka darr toh mujhe bhi rehta hai. Isliye main bhi unke mazak par chup reh gayi. Mujhe unke jaise kapde pehnne ya bolne ki tension se zyada bacchon ki school uniforms ki chinta karni hoti hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her clear priority of her children over fitting into the workplace culture using Lens B.
text: Ab aapko dheere-dheere yeh saaf ho raha hai ki wo dikhawa aapki priority nahi hai, kyunki aapki saari focus toh apne bacchon ki basic needs aur unke aage badhne par tिकी hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Meri niyat ekdum saaf hai apne bacchon ke liye. Par is poori thakan aur ghutan ke sath main aur kitne saal aisi extra shifts lagaungi? Mujhe kuch samajh nahi aa raha aage ka, koi raasta hai kya is exhaustion ko thoda jhelne ka?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offering small, manageable coping options that fit into her demanding routine without adding any new obligations.
text: Is mushkil waqt mein jab saari chinta aap hi jhel rahi hain, toh khud ko thoda sambhalne ke do raaste try kar sakte hain. Ek, lambi shift ke beech sirf do minute aakhein band karke baithna. Ya doosra, jab office mein koi aisi chubhnewali baat kahe, toh apne man mein yaad karna ki aap wahan unki validation ke liye nahi, bacchon ki fees ke liye aayi hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Do minute nikalna thik hai, par wo dusra wala... khud ko remind karana ki mera goal kya hai... yeh mujhe sahi lag raha hai. Par kaise focus karun exactaly jab wo log literally mere samne khade hoke has rahe hon?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walking her step by step through grounding herself during a difficult moment at work.
text: Pehle yeh karo ki agli baar jab aap unki koi bhi baat sunein, toh screen se nazar hataye bina ek lambi saans lo… phir apne man mein bas ek line dohrao: 'Mera maqsad mere bacchon ka aage ka bhavishya hai'… bas itna hi us waqt ke liye karna hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi hai. Main saans lungi aur apna maqsad dhyan mein laungi. Mujhe unke mazak ke aage apna farz aur apni izzat nahi bhulni chahiye. Par kabhi kabhi doubt hota hai ki kya main sach mein itni strong hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Systemic reframe normalizing workplace mockery as class/structural bias, rather than a reflection of her personal strength or capability.
text: Ye yaad rakhna zaruri hai ki aisi corporate jagahon mein accent ko lekar tanz kasna darasal structural elitism aur class bias ka hissa hota hai; ye aapki capability ya ek maa ke roop mein aapki mehnat par koi daag nahi lagata.

speaker: Seeker
text: Aapne mere dil ki baat keh di. Main itni bhi weak nahi hoon. Unka class bias unki problem hai, meri nahi. Par problem ye hai ki jab raat ko ghar aati hoon na, toh phir bilkul himmat nahi bachti.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her deep exhaustion returning once she drops her armor at the end of the day using Lens D.
text: Office mein itni himmat dikhane ke baad aur ghar wapas lautne tak, aapki saari bachi-kuchi energy jaise us lamba din kaatne mein hi poori tarah khatam ho jati hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Khatam hi ho jati hai poori. Raat ko dinner banane ka bhi mann nahi hota, jism dard karta hai, par bacchon ko bhookha toh nahi sula sakti. Toh phir se farz yaad aa jata hai aur main uth khadi hoti hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Asking an exploratory question to unpack what she wishes for herself when entirely depleted.
text: Jab us aakhiri thakan mein bhi aap thak kar dobara apne bacchon ke liye khadi hoti hain, toh theek us pal mein aapko khud ke liye sabse zyada kya wish karne ka mann karta hai?

speaker: Seeker

strategy_intent: Mirroring her longing for support within the constraints of her stigmatized reality using Lens C, with a soft pause.

strategy_intent: Validating her extraordinary sacrifice while acknowledging the painful reality she navigates daily.

text: Nahi aane de sakti. Agar mere bacchon ne kisi din mujhe rote hue dekh liya toh wo andar se darr jayenge. Main unka bachpan kisi darr ke saye mein kharab nahi karna chahti.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Highlighting the prolonged time duration and endlessness of dealing with the whispers while shielding the kids.

speaker: Seeker

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Naming the deep unexpressed feeling of isolation and fear of disappointing the family derived from masking their identity.
text: Mujhe lagta hai is lagatar acting aur duties ke bojh tale, tum ek bahut hi gahra isolation aur akelepan ka darr mehsus kar rahe ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Isolation toh itna hai ki bheed me bhi bilkul akela hoon. Kisi ko apne dil ki baat nahi bol sakta. Mujhe lagta tha main sab easily endure kar lunga, bas unki parvarish ache se ho jaye.

strategy_intent: Validating their suffocation as a culturally normal response to extreme structural pressure, separating their self-worth from the struggle.

strategy_intent: Reframing their suffocation as a systemic consequence of strict gender norms and societal structures, not a personal failing.
text: Yeh jo suffocation aur thakan tumhe mehsus ho rahi hai, yeh tumhare andar ki kami nahi, balki ek aisi society ka nateeja hai jo rigid gender roles aur fixed identities se thoda bhi alag hone par acceptance nahi deti.

speaker: Seeker
text: Societal rules toh main ek din me badal nahi sakta, ye baat main jaanta hoon. Par apne dimaag ko is shor aur ghutan se thoda kaise aaram du? Kuch toh rasta nikalna padega zinda rehne ke liye.

strategy_intent: Offering small, manageable suggestions to find identity-affirming moments within their demanding routine.
text: Hum abhi kuch choti cheezein try kar sakte hain jo tumhara time nahi lengi. Pehla, commute karte waqt earphones laga kar 5 minute aisa gaana sunna jo sirf tumhari identity se juda ho. speaker: Seeker
text: Kagaz wala idea thoda risky hai, ghar me koi khyaal bhi dekh le toh bawaal ho jayega. Par music wala idea acha lag raha hai. Earphones me apne pasand ka koi gaana sunna safe hai meri is hidden identity ke liye.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Guiding them step-by-step on how to implement the music suggestion safely.
text: Pehle yeh karo agle commute ke time akele mein apne earphones lagao… phir ek aasa gaana play karo jo tumhe sach me 'tum' hone ka ehsaas dilaye… bas itna hi aaj ke liye.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, bas music sunte waqt kisi ka taana ya call nahi aana chahiye. Par is ek chhote se waqt ke baad toh wapas usi reality me aana hai. Darr lagta hai ki uske baad ghutan zyada mehsus na ho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting the feared consequence of returning to the suffocating reality after experiencing a brief moment of freedom.

speaker: Supporter

strategy_intent: Mirroring the relational benefit that creating space for themselves builds a healthier dynamic for everyone around them.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Affirming their bravery in taking a step toward reclaiming a small piece of their identity despite the heavy structural barriers.
text: Yeh sunkar mujhe bahut tasalli mil rahi hai ki tumne apne liye thodi himmat jutayi hai, is dual identity aur exclusion ke darr ke aage khud ko fir se chunna ek bohot bravely liya gaya kadam hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai
text: Colleague ke tano ke baad dil kiya ki laptop band kar doon, par phir yaad aaya ki home loan ki kist baki hai aur divorcee hone ke baad 'log kya kahenge' ka darr hamesha rehta hai. Main apni LGBTQ identity aur apni insult dono bhool kar bas client deadlines poori karne mein lagi hoon taaki bacchon ki schooling mein koi rukawat na aaye. Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalize the mild risk by acknowledging their courage, then mirror the duty-based frame of prioritizing responsibilities over personal pain.
text: Bohot himmat lagti hai aise halaat mein bhi apni zimmedariyan nibhane mein. Bohot zor lagta hoga aise taane sunne ke baad bhi, yeh soch kar kaam karte rehna ki bacchon ki schooling aur home loan ki kist pehle aati hai, aur apni identity ya dard ke liye koi jagah nahi bachti.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai
text: Jagah hai hi kahan? Divorce ke baad log pehle hi baatein banate hain. Agar unhe meri reality pata chali toh pata nahi kya hoga. Bas deadlines meet karta hoon kyunki Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Softly name the implied fear of exposure and the societal backlash that keeps them hiding.
text: Yeh baat hamesha dimaag mein chalti rehti hogi ki agar kisi ko tumhari sacchai pata chal gayi, toh 'log kya kahenge' aur uska asar tumhari job ya bacchon par kaisa padega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Exactly. Society mein ek divorced insaan ko waise hi failures ki tarah dekhte hain. Upar se meri identity... mujhe har waqt ek mask pehan kar rakhna padta hai. Office mein WFH ke time bhi screen ke aage acting karni padti hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask about the emotional toll of continuously wearing a mask and acting in front of everyone.
text: Is tarah har din, chahe office ho ya WFH, ek mask pehan kar sabke saamne acting karne se andar kaisa feel hota hai tumko?

speaker: Seeker
text: Andar se poori tarah hollow lagta hai. Aisa lagta hai ki main apni khud ki life mein ek stranger hoon. Par phir bacchon ki taraf dekhta hoon toh lagta hai ki unka future secure karna hi mera farz hai. Apna dard toh sehna hi padega.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the prolonged duration of suffering in silence and prioritizing duty over self.
text: Itne lambe waqt se apne asli self ko chhupana aur sirf farz ke liye jeena, sach mein andar se bilkul khokhla aur thaka dene wala lagta hoga.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bahut thak gaya hoon. Roz sochta hoon ki kab tak akele yeh sab pull off kar paunga. Ek taraf office politics, doosri taraf EMIs aur divorce stigma. Koi samajhne wala nahi hai jisko main apna true self dikha sakun.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledge the stigma cue with a shorter restatement focusing on relational elements, ending with a soft pause.
text: Divorce aur tumhari identity ke is stigma ne tumhe apno ke beech bhi bahut akela kar diya hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai
text: Haan. Kabhi kabhi rona aata hai par bacchon ke saamne normal dikhna hota hai. Unki schooling pressure bhi bahut hai. Unko best dena hai mujhe. Isliye, Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Name the unexpressed exhaustion stemming from the relentless duty-based coping mechanism and hidden identity.
text: Lagta hai ki bacchon ke liye sab kuch perfect rakhne ki is koshish mein aur apni reality chhupane ke bojh se ek aisi deep exhaustion ikatthi ho gayi hai jo ab akele uthani bahut bhari pad rahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi pakda aapne. Thakan dimaag mein baith gayi hai. Par agar main ruk gaya, toh mere bacchon ka kya hoga? Main ek failed marriage de chuka hoon unhe, ab failed life nahi de sakta.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate the exhaustion as culturally normal for single breadwinners in metros, separating self-worth from societal definitions of failure.
text: Metro city mein akele single parent hokar, EMIs aur IT job ka pressure manage karna wakayi mein bahut exhausting hota hai; par is thakan ka matlab yeh nahi ki tum ek failed parent ho ya tumhara pyaar kam hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you yeh bolne ke liye. Par LGBTQ identity ko lekar society bahut cruel hai. Agar unhe pata chala, toh log bacchon ko taane marenge. Yeh dar mujhe andar hi andar kha raha hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Share a brief systemic anecdote to normalize the experience and highlight how common this hidden struggle is in their environment.
text: Corporate roles mein kaam karne wale kai single parents aur LGBTQ individuals mein yeh isolation bahut common hai—tum akele nahi ho, lekin koi khul kar isliye nahi bolega kyunki society aur 'log kya kahenge' ka darr hamesha rehta hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Wahi dar mujhe paralysis jaisa feel karata hai. Main apne bacchon ke liye ek healthy environment chahta hoon, par khud andar se mar raha hoon. Mujhe samajh nahi aa raha aage ka, aisi situation mein kya karoon?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offer 2 quick, non-intrusive options fitting their IT persona to help manage the immediate overwhelm without adding new obligations.
text: Yeh dar sach mein paralyse kar dene wala hai, par is thakan ko thoda kam karne ke liye hum do chhoti cheezein try kar sakte hain. Ek, jab bhi WFH mein panic ho, toh 2 minute ke liye screen band karke 5 lambi saansein lena. Dusra, din mein ek baar ek glass pani peete waqt sirf us movement par focus karna, bina kisi duty ke baare mein soche.

speaker: Seeker
text: Screen band karke saans lene wala idea practical lag raha hai. Mujhe WFH mein meetings ke beech mein anxiety spikes aate hain jab log personal baatein poonchte hain.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk through the suggested breathing exercise step by step in the context of a stressful meeting.
text: Pehle yeh karo… jab agli aisi meeting ho aur anxiety feel ho, toh apna mic mute karo, aankhein band karke ek lambi saans lo aur count karo... phir dheere se saans bahar chhodo... bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Okay, main yeh try karunga kal ki team sync call mein. Waise bhi camera mostly off hi rehta hai mera aajkal.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how they are actively finding ways to fulfill their professional duty while protecting themselves.
text: Camera off rakh kar aur is chote se breathing break ko use karke, tum apni is professional duty ko aur aaram se poora karne ka raasta nikal rahe ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, survive toh karna hi hai. Par sirf survive karne se kab tak chalega? Kabhi kabhi lagta hai shayad kisi aur city mein relocate kar jaoon jahan mujhe koi na jaanta ho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide a systemic reframe of the stressor to normalize their thoughts of relocation as a result of societal flaws, not personal failure.
text: Yeh soch aana bahut natural hai, kyunki humari society ka structure hi aisa hai jo alag identity wale single parents ko accept karne mein time lagata hai; yeh relocation ki soch society ki kami dikhati hai, tumhari nahi.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yeh sach hai ki society ki kami hai. Par bacchon ke school wagera shift karna abhi financially possible nahi hai. EMI ka burden already bahut zyada hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the financial constraint and feared consequences that keep them trapped in their current situation.
text: Financial burden aur EMI ki wajah se nayi jagah shift karne ka option abhi band lag raha hai, jisne tumhe is existing halaat mein phasne ka ehsaas kara diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai
text: Phasa hua hi hoon. Har mahine salary aati hai aur turant kist aur fees mein nikal jati hai. 'Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai', isliye bas kaam pe focus rakhta hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Probe how this concept of dharma specifically helps them stay afloat during high-pressure times.
text: Jab mahine ke aakhir mein itna pressure hota hai, toh yeh jo dharma aur duty ka ehsaas hai, yeh tumhe us mushkil waqt mein kaise himmat deta hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Dharma ka matlab mere liye bacchon ki hansi hai. Jab unhe acche school mein jaate dekhta hoon, toh lagta hai mere saare sacrifices worth it hain. Us pal ke liye mask pehan lene mein koi dikkat nahi hoti.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational bond with their children that fuels their endurance and coping.
text: Bacchon ke chehre ki khushi aur unka secure future dekhna hi tumhare liye wo taakat hai, jo is saare dard aur mask pehanne ko bhi sehne ke kaabil bana deta hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi kaha. Mere liye wahi sab kuch hain. Main apni identity ko thode aur saal suppress kar lunga, jab tak wo bade aur independent nahi ho jate. Tab tak mujhe thoda aur calm rehne ki aadat daalni padegi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the long-term horizon they are mentally preparing for and their determination to stay calm.
text: Agay ke in aane wale saalon tak khud ko shant rakhne aur apne bacchon ke bade hone ka intezaar karne ki tumne ek lambi planning kar li hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Planning nahi, majboori hai. Par ab lag raha hai ki beech beech mein un 2 minutes ke breaks lene se shayad ye safar thoda aasan ho jaye. Main completely tootne se toh bach jaunga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how taking these short breaks is becoming a new necessary duty to ensure their survival.
text: Majboori mein bhi apne liye woh 2 minute ke chote breaks nikalna ek nayi zaroori duty ban gayi hai jo tumhe is safar mein aage badhne mein madad karegi.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you. Main actually kabhi kisi se apni identity aur divorce dono ke baare mein is tarah bina dar ke baat nahi kar paya. Lagta tha judge hounga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledge the deep-seated fear of judgment they have held onto for so long.
text: Itne waqt se yeh darr hamesha rok leta tha ki kahin koi tumhe tumhari sacchai ke liye galat na samajh le ya judge na kar de.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, society mein ek darr toh rehta hi hai na, especially job wagera ko lekar. Par abhi aapse baat karke thoda halka feel ho raha hai. Dimaag mein ek clarity aa gayi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask them to vocalize the newly found clarity to close out the session positively.
text: Aaj aapse baat karke aur jo thoda halka feel hua hai, iske baad aage ke liye dimaag mein sabse badi clarity kya aayi hai?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai
text: Yahi ki mujhe abhi puri duniya se ladne ki zarurat nahi hai. Bas ek din mein ek meeting ko face karna hai aur apni saans par focus karna hai. Mere liye mental health ek luxury hai jo main afford nahi kar sakta, bas dharma hi mera asli sahara hai... par thoda breathing afford kar hi sakta hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Close with a structural normalizer confirming their small action is a valid survival tool against systemic pressure.
text: Bilkul, is heavy corporate aur societal pressure ke beech mein, apna dharma nibhate hue yeh choti choti saansein lene ki aadat dalna hi tumhara sabse bada aur practical support system banega.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai
text: Ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai, wahi duty din raat nibha raha hoon. Main exhausted hoon par rona kamzori lagega. Main afford nahi kar sakta ki bacchon ko koi kami mehsus ho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the duty frame where his pain and exhaustion are buried under the obligation to his children.
text: Akele dono bacchon ki parwarish aur office ki zimmedariyan nibhana aapka farz ban chuka hai, aur is ideal image ko barkarar rakhne ke chakkar mein aapko apne thakne ya dukh jatane ka waqt hi nahi mil raha.

speaker: Seeker

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the feared consequence of jeopardizing his children's stability if he reacts at work.
text: Aapke andar yeh darr baitha hua hai ki agar aapne office ki un baaton ka jawab diya, toh uska seedha aur bura asar aapke bacchon ki zarooraton aur unke future par padega.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai
text: Exactly. Aur logon ko toh mauka chahiye hota hai ungli uthane ka. Ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai taaki koi yeh na bole ki mard akele bacchon ko nahi pal sakta. Is chakkar mein meri apni feelings zero ho gayi hain.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Respond to the stigma cue by softly reflecting how he isolates himself to protect his family from judgment.
text: Dusron ke judgment aur taanon se apne parivar ko bachane ke liye, aapne apne dukh aur feelings ko puri tarah se andar daba liya hai...

speaker: Seeker
text: Daba hi liya hai. Main literally exhausted hoon andar se. Kabhi kabhi lagta hai aakhir kab tak chalega ye structural endurance. Office aur ghar ke is daily double shift me ek thaka hua robot ban gaya hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the time duration and the mechanical nature of his suffering.
text: Din raat bina ruke is dohari zindagi ko lagatar jeete jeete, aisa lag raha hai jaise aapka apna wajood piche chhut gaya ho aur bas ek machine bachi ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai
text: Insaan kahan raha main? Jab office meeting me mere pronunciation pe sab hase the, laga sab chhod ke apne town wapas chala jau. Par wahan relatives kahenge ki dekho, akele manage nahi kar paya. Ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai bas izzat bachane ke liye.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask an open question to explore how he manages these intense moments of humiliation internally.
text: Jab office mein aisi humiliating baatein hoti hain aur aapko apne dard ke sath samjhauta karna padta hai, us waqt aap khud ko tootne se kaise bachaate hain taaki ghar aakar sab normal dikha sakein?

speaker: Seeker
text: Bas washroom jata hu, muh dhota hu aur khud ko yaad dilata hu ki EMI deni hai, main financial precarity afford nahi kar sakta. Phir laptop band karke jab ghar aakar bacchon ko dekhta hu toh ek fake smile pehen leta hu.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror his duty framing of putting on a facade for his family despite inner turmoil.
text: Ghar ke darwaze ke bahar apna saara dard aur apmaan chhod kar, ek mazboot pita ka kirdaar nibhana aapki har roz ki ek aisi majboori ban chuki hai jise aap chah kar bhi nahi tod sakte.

speaker: Seeker
text: Majboori hi toh hai. Kisi ko bata nahi sakta ki main andar se darta hu. Lagta hai main kahin bich me hi na gir jau. Yeh thakan ab physical se zyada mental ho gayi hai. Ek ajeeb si suffocation hoti hai is 'perfect strong father' ke frame me.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Name the underlying exhaustion and fear of failing his duties.
text: Lagta hai ki is endless pressure aur duniya ke expectations ko poore karte karte, aap khud ko bilkul akela aur exhausted mehsus kar rahe hain, jaise ab yeh bojh sambhalna aapse sehan nahi ho raha.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul sehan nahi ho raha. Raat ko jab bacchon ko sota dekhta hu toh overthink karta hu ki agar mujhe kuch ho gaya toh unka kya hoga? Par apna darr aur weakness dikhana toh mardon ke liye allowed hi nahi hai hamari society me.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Respond to the stigma cue by softly reflecting the isolation caused by gender norms.
text: Unke future ki itni fikar hone ke bawajood, samaj ke banaye niyamon ki wajah se apne darr ko chupana aapko aur bhi akela kar raha hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai
text: Akela toh hu hi. Divorced admi ko waise hi log alag nazar se dekhte hain, upar se bacchon ki full custody. Corporate world samajhta hai ki mard hai toh office ka har stress easily le lega. Ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai, yahi meri reality ban gayi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalize his experience by sharing that single fathers often face this hidden double burden.
text: Akele bacchon ko palne wale mardon ki zindagi mein office aur ghar ke is double burden me pisna bahut common hai—sirf aapka akele ka dukh nahi hai, lekin koi ispe khul ke bolega nahi kyunki humare yahan mardon ko apna dard chupane ki hi training di jati hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi kaha aapne. Rona aur thakna toh sach me tabu maana jata hai mardon ke liye. Main bhul hi gaya tha ki main bhi ek insaan hu jisko ek lamba saans lene ki zarurat ho sakti hai. Mujhe lagta tha ki theek na feel karna meri failure hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate his exhaustion as normal and separate his worth from his ability to be a perfect unfeeling machine.
text: Aapka yeh andar tak thak jana aur kamzor mehsus karna bilkul normal hai. Bade shehar ke is IT mahol mein akele bacchon ko bada karna bohot himmat ka kaam hai. Aapki ahmiyat is baat se tay nahi hoti ki aap kitne perfect employee ya pita hain, balki us lagatar mehnat se dikhti hai jo aap har din kar rahe hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Mehnat toh main poori kar raha hu, din raat ek kar diya hai. Lekin kabhi na kabhi ye sabar ka bandh tootne lagta hai. Ye mockery wala incident mere liye ek breaking point tha. Main ab is constant suffocation ko aur carry nahi kar pa raha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror his realization that continuing this suppression could break him entirely.
text: Us din office mein hui us incident ne ek aisi aawaz di hai jisse aapko ab yeh darr lagne laga hai ki is tarah ghut-ghut kar akele chalna ab shayad aapse aur nahi ho payega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Ha, nahi ho pa raha mujhse. Aur main nahi chahta ki meri ye deep exhaustion mere bacchon pe utre aur main unpe irritate hu. Main theek hona chahta hu taki main unke sath genuinely has saku. Batao kya karoon is pressure ko thoda kam karne ke liye?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offer small, actionable suggestions to find pockets of rest within his heavy routine.
text: Yeh sukoon ki talash khud mein apne liye ek bohot acha kadam hai. Do choti aasan cheezein dekh sakte hain: kya aap subah WFH shuru karne se pehle 5 minute balcony mein akele baith sakte hain? Ya raat ko bacchon ke sone ke baad apni pasand ka koi shant gaana sun sakte hain?

speaker: Seeker
text: Balcony me subah thoda mushkil hai kyunki unka tiffin banana hota hai jaldi aur slack messages aane lagte hain. Par raat ko jab bache so jate hain aur ghar shant hota hai, tab main apna koi purana gaana phone me headset pe laga kar thoda time le sakta hu.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk him through executing the night-time music routine step by step.
text: Pehle yeh karo ki aaj raat jab bacche so jayein, toh laptop aur phone ki baki sab apps band kar do... phir headset pe sirf ek gaana apni pasand ka lagao aur aankhein band karke bas us dhun ko suno... bas itna hi aaj ke liye.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai
text: Itna shayad main kar pau. Halanki us time bhi dimaag me wahi accent ka taana aur next sprint ki deadlines ghumti rehti hain. Ek single parent ko double mehnat karni padti hai hamesha, par shayad 5 minute nikalne se office ya ghar ka aasman nahi girega.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframe his guilt around resting as a systemic issue, normalizing breaks as fuel for his duties.
text: Aapke dimaag mein jo lagatar yeh deadlines ghumti hain, woh sirf society ka banaya hua 'hamesha kaam karte rehne wale pita' ka frame hai jisme aaram allowed nahi. Apne liye yeh 5 minute lena koi laziness nahi, balki khud ko dobara khada karne ka zaroori hissa hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Khud ko dobara khada karna... acha perspective diya aapne. Mujhe hamesha lagta tha apne aaram ke baare me sochna matlab bacchon ki mehnat se cheating karna. Lekin sach me main itna tut chuka hu toh unko ek healthy baap kaise de paunga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror his shift in perspective regarding rest and duty.
text: Ab tak aap apne sukoon ko apne farz ke khilaf maante the, par ab aap saaf dekh pa rahe hain ki thoda thehrav aapko bacchon ke liye ek aur behtar aur shant pita banayega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bina shant hue main waise bhi unki help nahi kar raha. Aur office me bhi mujhe dhyan rakhna hoga ki meri identity sirf mera desi accent ya mera code nahi hai. Main unke taano ko apne self-worth pe haavi hone de raha tha.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how he is separating his identity from workplace mockery.
text: Aap ab un colleagues ke behuda mazak aur apne astitva ke beech ek line draw kar rahe hain, jahan aapki ahmiyat kisi meeting ke taano se tay nahi hoti.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul. Main single-handedly do bacchon ko IT job ke sath metro me raise kar raha hu, ye apne aap me bada kaam hai. Samaj ya manager kya sochta hai, ab I shouldn't care so much. Main EMI aur bacchon ki life theek karne wahan jata hu.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Help him formulate a mental anchor to protect his peace during future workplace conflicts.
text: Jab bhi aage chalkar office ki aisi baatein ya kisi ka taana aapka confidence hilane ki koshish kare, toh apni is achievement aur nayi soch ko yaad dilane ke liye aap khud se us waqt kya kahenge?

speaker: Seeker
text: Main khud se kahunga ki meri life ki mehnat unki samajh se bahar hai, aur main apne bacchon ke liye poori tarah se kabil hu. Mujhe unke approval ki koi zarurat nahi hai, main bas apna kaam karunga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate his new mindset as powerful and essential for maintaining his balance.
text: Yeh soch wakai bahut taqatwar aur zaroori hai. Itne akelepan aur pressure ke beech aisi clarity dikhana sabit karta hai ki aapne apne farzon ke sath-sath ab apne swabhimaan ki bhi raksha karna shuru kar diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you. Mujhe sach me is clear conversation ki bohot zarurat thi. Hamesha sirf ek ATM aur provider ki tarah khud ko dekhta tha. Aaj pehli baar laga ki koi mere dukh ko bhi bina ungli uthaye ek insaan ki tarah dekh raha hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how feeling heard without judgment has brought relief to his exhaustion.
text: Is poore safar mein bina kisi judgment ke sune jaane se, aapke andar chupa hua woh ghutan mehsus kar raha ek akela pita aaj thoda halka mehsus kar raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bohot halka. Kal se subah wahi rush hoga, tiffin banega aur deadlines hongi. Par ab mere paas raat ke 5 minute me mera purana gaana hoga. Aur ye ehsaas hoga ki main sirf thaka hua hu, main haara hua nahi hu.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror his forward-looking resilience and the new boundary he set for his peace.
text: Kal ke usi busy din aur purane challenges ka samna karne ke liye, apni mehnat par yeh naya garv aur un paanch minuton ka sukoon, aapko ek lamba aur positive thehrav dega.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera 'farz' kehta hai ki mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai
text: Mera 'farz' kehta hai ki mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai kyunki in bacchon ka future mujh par hi tika hai. Yeh urban isolation aur logon ke taane itne chubhte hain, par double shift karna padta hai izzat bachane ke liye.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the Seeker's duty frame to validate her core coping mechanism.
text: Tumhare liye sabse bada maqsad apne bacchon ka bhavishya surakshit rakhna hai, aur us farz ke aage tum apni khud ki bhari thakan ko bilkul bhula deti ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, bhulana hi padta hai. Agar main thak gayi toh inka kya hoga? Society toh waise hi divorce stigma ka tag laga ke baithi hai, ek galti ki aur sab izzat mitti mein mil jayegi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflect the underlying fear of consequence regarding society's judgment.
text: Aisa lagta hai ki duniya ki is sakht nazar aur tag ki wajah se tum us darr mein jeeti ho ki tumhari thodi si bhi thakan se bacchon par ungli uthegi.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera 'farz' kehta hai ki mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai
text: Logon ka toh kaam hi hai bolna, isliye mujhe sab strong banke dikhana padta hai. Andar se main completely exhausted hoon, kabhi kabhi literally rone ka man karta hai, par tabhi mera 'farz' kehta hai ki mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Address the stigma cue gently with a short duration-focused restatement and a soft pause.
text: Itne waqt se akele is bhaari thakan ko uthate hue aur sabse chupate hue...

speaker: Seeker
text: Chupana padta hai kyunki agar city mein kisi ko pata chala main tut rahi hoon, toh office wale bhi differently treat karenge. Meri double shift hi meri pehchaan aur majboori dono ban gayi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Ask an open question to explore the emotional weight of this continuous hiding after two restatements.
text: Jab tum poora din aise logo se aur society ki baaton se ladkar wapas bacchon ke paas aati ho, tab andar se kaisa feel hota hai tumhein?

speaker: Seeker
text: Andar se bas guilt aur emptiness hoti hai. Lagta hai main inke liye enough nahi hoon. Unki izzat ke liye main bhag toh rahi hoon, par theek se waqt nahi de paati unhe is double shift ki wajah se.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the relational dynamic between her sacrifice and her feelings of guilt towards her children.
text: Bacchon ke liye tumhara yeh pyaar aur fikr itni gehri hai ki tum unki bahari izzat bachane mein khud se aur unke sath bitaane wale waqt se hi door ho gayi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi kaha aapne. Unki izzat ke chakkar mein maine apni saans lene ki jagah bhi khatam kar li hai. Mera sharir roz bolta hai ruk jao, par dimaag sunta nahi. Yeh deep exhaustion ab jaan le rahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Name the underlying exhaustion behind the duty-based framing.
text: Lagta hai ki is double responsibility aur is akelepan ne tumhare andar ek bohot bhari thakan paida kar di hai, kyunki apne farz ko nibhate nibhate ab tumhara sharir jawab de raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Jawab toh de raha hai, but options kahan hain? Main ek single mother hoon is bade se shehar mein. Yeh urban isolation aise khaata hai ki padosi bhi judge karne ke liye hi apko dekhte hain.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate the exhaustion as culturally normal for single mothers in metros and separate her worth from the societal judgment.
text: Yeh akelapan aur darr jo tum mehsoos kar rahi ho wo bilkul natural hai; badhe sheharon mein akele bacchon ko palna aur is divorce stigma ko jhelna waise hi bahut harsh hota hai, aur tumhara is thakan mein hona tumhari maa hone ki lagan par koi sawaal nahi uthata.

speaker: Seeker
text: Par yeh baat samaj ka koi aur insaan nahi samajhta. Sabko meri bas galtiyan dikhti hain ya kamiyaan. Mujhe khud itna guilt lagta hai ki kya main sach mein ek buri maa ban rahi hoon is roz ki bhaag-daud mein?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Use a brief systemic anecdote to normalize the relentless guilt placed on single mothers.
text: Single mothers ke safar mein yeh guilt wala pattern bahut common hai jahan har chhoti baat par khud ko blame kiya jata hai — sirf tumhara yeh haal nahi hai, lekin koi bahar khul ke bolega nahi kyunki sab apni thakan chupate hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Aapne mere dil ki baat keh di. Weakness chupate chupate main khud andar se itni weak pad gayi hoon. Mujhe iss guilt aur thakan se ab thoda free hona hai, mujhe theek hona hai... ab kya karoon main?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide small, completable options matching her persona and exhaustion level.
text: Aage badhne ke liye do chhote tareeqe hain jo bina zyada waqt liye tumhari routine mein aa sakte hain—ya toh raat ko bacchon ke sone ke baad 2 minute chai ka cup akele shanti se peena, ya din mein ek baar aayine mein dekh kar khud se kehna ki 'main inke liye apni taraf se apna best kar rahi hoon'. Kaun sa option thik lagta hai?

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: mera 'farz' kehta hai ki mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai
text: Aayine wala shayad kar sakti hoon. Chai toh main thakan mein pee hi nahi paati, seedha bed pe girti hoon. Khud ko tasalli dena zaroori hai kyunki mera 'farz' kehta hai ki mere paas thakne ka waqt nahi hai, par dil wakai aaram mangta hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walk her through the chosen mirror suggestion step-by-step.
text: Toh phir aaj raat se pehle yeh karo… sone se theek pehle jab tum washroom jao, toh aayine mein khud ki aankhon mein dekh kar bas ek gehri saans lena… aur apni ki hui din bhar ki mehnat ko sachi me recognize karna… bas itna hi aaj ke liye.

speaker: Seeker
text: Ek baar aayine mein dekhna aur recognize karna... Koshish karungi main. Halanki society ki divorce wali nazar aayine mein bhi peecha nahi chhodti, lagta hai jaise koi wahan bhi mujhe judge kar raha hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Systemically reframe the internalized societal judgment.
text: Aksar hum dekhte hain ki samaj ek single maa par 'perfect' hone ka itna bhari pressure dal deta hai ki hum unhi ki judgmental nazron se khud ko dekhne lagte hain, aur yahi structure humara apna darr ban jata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Yes, exactly! Woh judgmental nazar mere dimaag mein deep bas gayi hai. Main poora din metro mein aur office mein logo ko avoid karti hoon isi wajah se, bas sar jhuka ke apna kaam karti hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how the fear of judgment dictates her actions in the city.
text: Us judge kiye jaane ka darr itna haavi ho chuka hai ki tumne apni poori energy logo se bachne aur bacchon ki image safe rakhne mein laga di hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, bas image safe rakhti hoon taaki bacchon ko school mein ya society mein koi taana na maare. Par is process mein main completely jal gayi hoon, mera wajood bas ek working machine jaisa reh gaya hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror how she lost connection to herself while defending against society.
text: Bacchon ko taano se door rakhne ki is ladaai mein tumne apna khud ka connection apni jaruraton aur aaram ke sath poori tarah se tod diya hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Meri jaruratein toh matter hi nahi karti ab kisi ke liye bhi. Lekin theek hai, aaj aayine ke samne apne aap se yeh connection wapas jodne ki ek choti si try zaroor karungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the transformation from running purely on duty to allowing some space for herself.
text: Ab tumhara yeh farz tumhe sirf doosron ke darr se bhagne par majboor nahi kar raha, balki apne khud ke dhyan ke liye bhi thoda waqt nikalne ki ijazat de raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad ijazat leni padegi ab khud se. Waqt nahi hai, par ek minute toh nikal hi sakti hoon, varna main bacchon ke aage jake kaam bhi nahi aa paungi agar physically crash ho gayi toh.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Prompt a concrete statement of self-affirmation to solidify the action step.
text: Jab tum aayine mein dekh kar khud se pehli baar yeh kahogi ki tum is mushkil waqt mein bhi apna best de rahi ho, toh us lamhe mein kaisa mehsoos hone ki umeed hai?

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad thoda ajeeb lagega start mein, par relief bhi milega. Ek assurance jaisa ki kisi ne toh dekha meri us sacchi mehnat ko, chahe wo main khud hi kyun na hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror the significance of this shift over the long duration of her exhaustion.
text: Itni lambi exhaustion aur akelepan ke baad, apna khud ka sahara banna aur us relief ko apne andar feel karna apne aap mein ek bohot bada badlav hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Badlav laana toh padega hi ab. Divorce stigma wali in society ki nazaron se toh umeed hi nahi karni chahiye ki wo sahara denge ya meri mehnat ko respect karenge.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Provide a systemic reframe on why single parents need to shift this locus of validation away from society.
text: Sach mein, jab tak society apne in purane stigmas aur prejudices se aage nahi badhti, tab tak working single mothers ko apni self-worth khud hi tay karni padti hai taaki yeh unfair system unhe thaka na sake.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bohot valid point hai. Agar unke hisaab se chali toh bas dar-dar ke jeena padega. Mera farz bacchon ko strong banana hai, aur uske liye mujhe bhi andar se strong banna hi hoga.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirror her duty frame again, now infused with positive clarity.
text: Tumhara farz nibhane ka definition ab logo se darne ki jagah tumhari apni andar ki mazbooti aur bacchon ke samne ek clear example set karne mein badal raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thank you for saying this. Definition change ho rahi hai sach mein. Main apne kaam mein honest hoon, aur mere bacche meri priority hain. Un taanon ko main aur apne upper haavi nahi hone dungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validate her new perspective and readiness.
text: Mujhe tumhari in baton mein ek bohot pakka vishwas dikh raha hai; itni mushkilon ke bawajood apne bacchon ke liye is dedication ko banaye rakhna, tumhari ehmiyat ko samaj ke kisi bhi tag se bohot upar rakhta hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Ab thoda clear samajh aa raha hai. Thakan toh physically rahegi double shift ki wajah se, par mental bojh thoda kam ho gaya hai aapse baat karke. Aaj raat pakka mirror ke saamne wo minute nikalungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: A relational restatement to conclude warmly on her self-bond.
text: Aaj tumne khud ko thoda aur samajh kar, apne dimaag ki us purani thakan aur bacchon ke prati lagav ke beech apne liye ek bohot sacha raasta dhoond liya hai.
